pattern: A size-k min-heap keeps only the k largest values seen so far, so after one pass its smallest element is the kth largest overall, giving the answer without fully sorting the array.
hints: You want the kth largest by value, counting duplicates, not the kth distinct value.
Maintain a min-heap holding at most k elements; whenever it grows past k, discard its smallest, so it always retains the k biggest values seen.
After processing every element, the root of that k-element min-heap is exactly the kth largest; quickselect with a random pivot is an alternative average-linear approach.

edge_inputs.description: second largest in an unsorted array
all identical values, any k gives that value

stress.description: large random array with k chosen across the valid range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randint(-10000, 10000) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, n)
    return [nums, k]
stress.note: k spans 1..n so both extremes and middle ranks are exercised
